Japanese: ２月２５日　午前　９時４７分 地方裁判所　被告人第２控え室
English: February 25, 9:47 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 2

Japanese: （ともえさんも、あかねちゃんも いない、控え室‥‥か）
English: (This is the Defendant Lobby all right... but there's no defendant.)

Japanese: （今朝から、ともえさんに 連絡がとれない）
English: (I've been trying to reach Lana all morning. Where could she be?)

Japanese: （これは、どうやら‥‥）
English: (And where's Ema, for that matter? It almost seems as if...)

Japanese: ‥‥我々の知らないところで、 何か、動きがあったようだな。
English: Something's been happening behind the scenes...

Japanese: み‥‥‥御剣！
English: Edgeworth!

Japanese: ‥‥キミのコトだ。すでに、 見当はついているのだろう。
English: Knowing you, you've already figured it out...

Japanese: 《７７７７７７７》の ＩＤナンバーについて。
English: Who the owner of the "7777777" ID number is.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 （コイツも、つきとめたみたいだ）
English: Well... I have a pretty strong hunch.

Japanese: （あのナンバーの“主”を‥‥）
English: (Looks like I'm not the only one who's figured it out.)

Japanese: きのうの審理で、判決が 下されなかった理由は、ただ１つ。
English: You know, the only reason this trial didn't reach a verdict yesterday...

Japanese: このＩＤ使用記録のリストに、 ギモンの余地が残っていたからだ。
English: is because there was still room for doubt on this ID record.

Japanese: このナンバーが“彼”のもので あれば‥‥そのギモンは消える。
English: If that number does belong to whom you suspect, then no doubt will remain.

Japanese: 警察局長には、“公式な容疑”は かかっていないからな。
English: After all, he hasn't been officially charged with anything.

Japanese: ‥‥今のところは、だよ。
English: True... Not yet.

Japanese: とにかく、すべての ギモンが消える以上‥‥
English: In any event, once all doubt has been removed from that list, I can call for a ruling

Japanese: 開廷後、５分。私は、被告人に 対する判決を求めることができる。
English: on the defendant. Five minutes after the trial starts... Lana will be found guilty.

Japanese: でも！　それは まちがった判決だ！
English: But she didn't do it!

Japanese: そう言うと思った。 ‥‥だから、話を聞きにきた。
English: I figured you'd say as much. That's why I came here... to hear what you have to say.

Japanese: （コイツがそんなコト言うの、 初めて聞いたな‥‥）
English: (This is the first time he's ever done something like this...)

Japanese: 彼女は、何かをかくしている。 真実を知るためには‥‥
English: Lana's hiding something, and the only way we'll ever know the truth...

Japanese: それを引きずり出すしかない。
English: is to draw it out of her.

Japanese: “真実”‥‥だと？
English: The "truth"?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》だ。 すべては、そこにつながっている。
English: Everything goes back to the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: バカなッ！　宝月　巴の審理は 今日が最後なのだ！
English: Don't be stupid! Today's the last day of the trial.

Japanese: 過去をふりかえっている ヒマなど、ない！
English: We don't have time to reminisce about the past!

Japanese: おまえしだいだよ、御剣。
English: That depends on you.

Japanese: 判決が下れば‥‥真実を知る チャンスは、永久に失われる！
English: If she's found guilty, you'll lose your only chance to find out what really happened.

Japanese: 考えておこう。
English: I'll think about it.

Japanese: それでは‥‥法廷で会おう。
English: See you in court, Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥今こそ、つきとめるんだ）
English: (This is it.)

Japanese: （なぜ彼女は、局長の“命令”に 逆らうコトができないのか‥‥）
English: (If I'm ever going to find out what Chief Gant has on her, it's now!)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第９法廷
English: February 25, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 9

Japanese: これより、宝月　巴の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Ms. Lana Skye.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側、準備完了している。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: いつもならば、ここで検事による 冒頭弁論があるのですが‥‥
English: Normally this is when the prosecution puts forth its opening statement,

Japanese: その前に、警察局長より 緊急提案があるそうです。
English: but before that, the police chief has a proposal to make.

Japanese: （巌徒局長‥‥！）
English: (Chief Gant...!)

Japanese: や。みんな。おはよ。 どうよチョーさん。泳いでる？
English: Mornin' folks. How's everyone doing? Hey, Udgey. Been back to the pool yet?

Japanese: いやいや。シゴトにおぼれて アップアップしとりますよ。
English: No, I've been drowning enough as it is in my work.

Japanese: あ。それ。ウマいなチョーさん。 かなわないや。イヤまったく。
English: Ho! That's a good one! Don't think I can top that!

Japanese: それで‥‥局長。 “提案”というのは？
English: If you don't mind me asking, Chief, exactly what is this "proposal" of yours?

Japanese: トモエちゃん‥‥被告人がね。
English: Lana... that is to say, the defendant,

Japanese: どうしても、言っておきたい コトがある、って言いだしてさ。
English: has asked me if she could speak directly to the court.

Japanese: （ともえさんが‥‥？）
English: (She wants to do what!?)

Japanese: いろいろ。手っ取り早いと思って。 ちょっとさ。聞いてみてよ。
English: Having heard what she intends to say, I feel she should be granted her request.

Japanese: ‥‥ソンはないと思うんだよねェ。
English: In the end, it should save everyone a lot of time and trouble.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、 どういうコトだろうか。被告人‥‥
English: What's this all about, defendant?

Japanese: ‥‥申し上げたい内容は、 ヒトコトで済みます。
English: I'd just like to make one simple request, and I'll be finished.

Japanese: と、いいますと？
English: Well then? What's your request?

Japanese: 即刻、審理を終了して いただきたいのです。
English: Your Honor, I'd like you to put an immediate end to this trial.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですか！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: ‥‥私は、全面的に 犯行を認めます。
English: I confess to all charges against me.

Japanese: 本年２月２１日、地方検事局の 地下駐車場において‥‥
English: On February 21 of this year, I murdered Detective Bruce Goodman...

Japanese: 捜査官・多田敷　道夫を 殺害いたしました。
English: in the underground parking lot of the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥‥ なんですッてエエエェェッ！
English: No, Lana!

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: You can't!

Japanese: 被告人の発言は、 弁護側の主張と反しています！
English: Your Honor. The defendant's claim does not change the defense's plea!

Japanese: ‥‥それならば、成歩堂弁護士。
English: In that case, Mr. Wright,

Japanese: 私は、あなたを 自分の弁護人とは認めません。
English: I no longer require your services.

Japanese: と、ともえさん‥‥！
English: But Lana!

Japanese: 裁判長。これより、私は、 いっさいの弁護を受け入れません。
English: Your Honor. I hereby forfeit my right to an attorney.

Japanese: 幸か不幸か、直接的な証拠品は まだ、足りないかもしれません。
English: The prosecution may lack direct evidence against me,

Japanese: しかし。状況的な証拠ならば、 検察側の立証はじゅうぶんです。
English: but it has sufficiently proven its case through testimony and circumstantial evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥判決をおねがいいたします。
English: I would like you to render your verdict now, if you please.

Japanese: ふ、ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: たしかに被告人には、代理人である 弁護士を拒否することができます。
English: Well, the defendant certainly has the right to self representation...

Japanese: ‥‥ただ今の被告人の主張は、 法の定めに反するものではない‥‥
English: Her request is legally valid, although this is an unprecedented situation.

Japanese: 前代未聞の事態ですが‥‥どうやら 審理の必要は、ないようです。
English: Indeed, it appears there's no further need to continue this trial...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人にとっては、不本意な 結果となったようですが。
English: even if Mr. Wright may feel otherwise.

Japanese: （そ、そんな‥‥バカな！）
English: (This can't be happening!)

Japanese: それでは被告人、宝月　巴に 判決を言いわたします！
English: It appears the time for the verdict has arrived. This court finds the defendant...

Japanese: お待ちいただこう、裁判長。
English: One moment, Your Honor.

Japanese: み‥‥‥御剣検事！
English: M-Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: ‥‥検察側の立証は、 まだ終了していない。
English: The prosecution has not yet proven the defendant guilty beyond reasonable doubt.

Japanese: 今の時点での判決に、 我々は同意できないッ！
English: Any ruling at this stage would certainly be premature.

Japanese: ‥‥あのさあ、御剣ちゃん。
English: Come now, Worthy.

Japanese: 今、キミ。ムズカシイ時期なワケ。 わかるでしょ？　オトナなんだし。
English: I understand this is a difficult time for you,

Japanese: これ以上。モンダイを起こさない ほうが、身のためだと思わない？
English: but why don't you just be a good little boy and keep your mouth shut, hmm?

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 見くびらないでもらいたいな ‥‥局長殿。
English: I don't think I care for your tone... Chief Gant.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: What?

Japanese: 過去のアヤマチをかくすために、 新しいイツワリをくりかえす‥‥
English: Creating another fabrication to cover up your past mistakes...

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 私はそれほど、愚かではないのだ。
English: Sorry, but I'm no longer the naive little "boy" you would have me be.

Japanese: 被告人の、いきなりの自供‥‥
English: With this sudden confession from the defendant...

Japanese: ウラで何か“取引”が あったとしか考えられないが？
English: It's obvious to me some kind of deal was struck behind the scenes.

Japanese: 取引‥‥ねェ。キミのトクイな ゲームじゃないの？　それ。
English: Some kind of "deal," hm? Not everyone operates as you do... Worthy.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 私のココロも決まったようだ。
English: Hmph. I thought so.

Japanese: 裁判長。最初の証人を変更したい。
English: Your Honor. The prosecution would like to change its first witness.

Japanese: と、いいますと‥‥？
English: Oh? To whom...?

Japanese: 検察側の証人は‥‥‥ 宝月　茜！
English: As its first witness, the prosecution would like to call... Ms. Ema Skye!

Japanese: 彼女の入廷を要請するッ！
English: I request the court hears her testimony!

Japanese: 御剣検事！　私は自己弁護のケンリ を行使しているのです。
English: Mr. Edgeworth! I am exercising my right to self representation.

Japanese: これ以上の審理は‥‥
English: I don't think we need to contin-

Japanese: 知ったことではない。
English: I don't care what you think, Ms. Skye.

Japanese: “真実”を暴くことは‥‥ 時に、ヒゲキを引き起こす。
English: The exposure of truth sometimes results in tragedy...

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。 それ以上のヒゲキが存在する。
English: However! No matter how tragic the truth may be,

Japanese: それは‥‥ “真実”から目をそらすこと、だ。
English: it would be an even greater tragedy... to avert one's eyes from it.

Japanese: 検察側の要請を認めます！ ‥‥よろしいですな？　巌徒局長。
English: Very well. The court shall grant the prosecution's request.
That's okay with you, right Chief Gant?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣ちゃん‥‥ 後悔するよ。かならず。
English: Worthy... You'll live to regret this. Mark my words.

Japanese: ‥‥宝月　茜を 入廷させていただきたい。
English: Ms. Ema Skye. Please take the stand.

Japanese: （御剣‥‥ カクゴを決めたようだな！）
English: (Looks like Edgeworth has decided to take the horse by the reins!)

Japanese: それでは、証人。名前と職業を。
English: Now then, witness. Please state your name and occupation.

Japanese: あ、あの。あたし‥‥ 名前は、宝月　茜です。
English: Um... M-my name is Ema. Ema Skye.

Japanese: 職業は‥‥お姉ちゃんの妹で、 カガク捜査官をめざしています。
English: My occupation? I'm Lana's little sister, and I want to be a scientific investigator!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、今から２年前。
English: Two years ago...

Japanese: 《青影事件》と呼ばれる事件で、 殺人者・青影　丈に遭遇した‥‥
English: you encountered the serial killer Joe Darke, of the "Joe Darke Killings."

Japanese: マチガイないだろうか。
English: Is this correct?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: あたし‥‥いっしょうけんめい、 忘れようとしてるんですけど‥‥
English: I'm trying my hardest to forget about that, though...

Japanese: まことに 申しわけないのだが‥‥
English: I'm sorry,

Japanese: これから、そのキオクを もう一度、たどっていただく。
English: but I'm afraid I'm going to have to ask you to recall those events one more time.

Japanese: しかし。この場で審理すべきは、 多田敷捜査官の殺害事件です。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Please remember this trial concerns the murder of Detective Goodman.

Japanese: ‥‥２年前に解決した事件が、 それほど重要なのですかな？
English: Is an incident that was resolved two years ago really all that relevant?

Japanese: 重要なのだ。
English: Yes. It most certainly is.

Japanese: わ、わかりました！
English: W-well okay then!

Japanese: （弱ェ！）
English: (He sure gave in fast...)

Japanese: それでは‥‥２年前。
English: Now...

Japanese: あなたが体験したことを 証言していただこう。
English: please testify about what happened to you two years ago.

Japanese: （‥‥ついに、 過去への旅が始まる‥‥）
English: (The trip to yesteryear has finally begun...)

Japanese: （旅の終着点は‥‥きっと。 今回の事件の“真相”だ！）
English: (It's bound to lead to the truth behind this trial!)

Japanese: ～２年前の事件～
English: -- Two Years Ago --

Japanese: あの日‥‥警察局のオフィスで お姉ちゃんを待っていました。
English: I was waiting in my sister's office that day.

Japanese: ハンニンが逃げてきて‥‥ ヒトジチにされたんです。
English: A man came running in, and took me hostage.

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事さんが、あたしを 助けてくれたんですけど‥‥
English: Neil Marshall rescued me,

Japanese: そのとき、一瞬見た光景は、 今も忘れません！
English: but I'll never forget what I saw that instant!

Japanese: ハンニンがナイフを振り上げて、 検事さんのムネに‥‥！
English: The man raised up his knife, and... and stabbed Mr. Marshall in the chest...!

Japanese: ‥‥本当に、 ブジでよかったですな。
English: It's a good thing you weren't harmed.

Japanese: あたし、キゼツしちゃって‥‥ よく覚えていないんです。
English: I passed out... I don't remember much.

Japanese: 当然でしょう。 しかし‥‥この証言が、いったい。
English: That's understandable. However... please tell me, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官の殺人事件と、 どう関わってくるのですかな？
English: What does this testimony have to do with Detective Goodman's murder?

Japanese: それは、これから アキラカになるだろう。
English: That will soon become apparent, Your Honor.

Japanese: （‥‥なんのコンキョもないのに、 よくあんなコト、言えるよな）
English: (You've got to admire him for his courage, considering he has no evidence...)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ ‥‥尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well! The defense may begin its cross examination.

Japanese: ～２年前の事件～
English: -- Two Years Ago --

Japanese: あの日‥‥警察局のオフィスで お姉ちゃんを待っていました。
English: I was waiting in my sister's office that day.

Japanese: ハンニンが逃げてきて‥‥ ヒトジチにされたんです。
English: A man came running in, and took me hostage.

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事さんが、あたしを 助けてくれたんですけど‥‥
English: Neil Marshall rescued me,

Japanese: そのとき、一瞬見た光景は、 今も忘れません！
English: but I'll never forget what I saw that instant!

Japanese: ハンニンがナイフを振り上げて、 検事さんのムネに‥‥！
English: The man raised up his knife, and... and stabbed Mr. Marshall in the chest...!

Japanese: そのときのこと、絵に描いたのに。 なくなっちゃったみたいですね‥‥
English: I drew a picture of that scene once... but it seems to have been lost.

Japanese: （‥‥あかねちゃんに、 ウソをつく“理由”はない）
English: (Ema doesn't have any reason to lie...)

Japanese: （しかし。ともえさんには その“理由”があるんだ‥‥）
English: (but Lana sure does.)

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、情報を 引き出していこう！）
English: (I need to get Ema to tell me as much about this as she can!)

Japanese: ２年前‥‥被告人・宝月　巴さんは 警察局の捜査官だったんですね。
English: Two years ago, the defendant was a detective at the Police Department, correct?

Japanese: ええ。副主席捜査官でした。 あの！　‥‥カッコよかったです。
English: Yes. She was second in command under Chief Detective Gant.
My sister... She was the best detective ever!

Japanese: よく、おぼえておりますぞ。
English: Yes, I remember.

Japanese: 当時の、巌徒主席捜査官とコンビを 組んで、ごカツヤクでしたな。
English: Chief Detective Gant and Ms. Skye used to be quite the pair.

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、巌徒　海慈と オフィスも共にしていた。
English: I believe they shared the same office.

Japanese: はい。あたし‥‥いつも、 お姉ちゃんのデスクにすわって‥‥
English: That's right.
I'd always sit at my sister's desk,

Japanese: あのオルガンをひいてみたいな、 って思っていたんです。
English: and dream about playing that organ.

Japanese: ‥‥あの日も、そうでした。
English: I wanted to play it that day, too...

Japanese: あれは、２年前。検事局と警察局の 式典が行われた日だった‥‥
English: The Police Department and the Prosecutor's Office held a ceremony that day.

Japanese: おシゴトが終わったら、ゴハンを 食べに行くヤクソクでした。
English: Lana promised to take me to dinner after she finished her work.

Japanese: “ハンニン”というのは‥‥
English: A "man"...?

Japanese: 青影　丈っていうヒトでした。
English: Yes, Joe Darke.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 サツジンハンだ、って。
English: He was a... a serial killer.

Japanese: あの日。式典のかたわら、 青影の事情聴取が行われていた。
English: Joe Darke was brought in for questioning on the day of that ceremony.

Japanese: ‥‥我々は、彼の容疑をかためる のに、必死だったのだ。
English: We were desperate to get anything on him that would lead to an arrest.

Japanese: そのスキをついて‥‥ 青影が逃走したんですね。
English: When he saw his chance... he fled the room, right?

Japanese: 取調べ室から逃げたヤツは、 エレベータに乗りこんだ。
English: Upon fleeing the room, Darke proceeded to take the elevator.

Japanese: あわてていたのだろう。 そのエレベータは、のぼりだった。
English: He must have been in a panic because the elevator was going up.

Japanese: そして‥‥巌徒・宝月のオフィスに 逃げこんでしまったのだ。
English: Then he ran into Skye and Gant's office.

Japanese: 部屋のソトが、なんとなく さわがしくなって‥‥あたし。
English: There was a lot of noise coming from outside, so I...

Japanese: ドアを開けて、のぞいてみました。 そこに‥‥あのヒトがいたんです。
English: opened up the door to have a look. That's when I saw... Him.

Japanese: なぜ、検事さんが‥‥？
English: What was the prosecutor doing there?

Japanese: ‥‥あの日。 青影　丈の事情聴取には‥‥
English: That day, there were two people present during Darke's questioning:

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈と罪門　直斗検事の ２人があたった。
English: Detective Damon Gant, and Prosecutor Neil Marshall.

Japanese: （巌徒も、か‥‥）
English: (Gant was there too...?)

Japanese: 罪門　直斗はその日、 検事局賞を受けたばかりだった。
English: Neil Marshall had just received the King of Prosecutors award.

Japanese: 若く、情熱に燃えて‥‥式典のあと 取調べ室に直行したという。
English: Young and dedicated, he went straight to the questioning room after the ceremony.

Japanese: それで、逃走した青影のあとを まっ先に追いかけたワケですな。
English: I assume that would also be why he was the first to run after Darke.

Japanese: あのオトコにつかまれて、あたし。 もう、ダメだって思ったんです。
English: When Darke grabbed me, I... I thought I was as good as dead.

Japanese: そこへ‥‥罪門検事が かけつけてきたワケだね？
English: And that's when Prosecutor Marshall came running in?

Japanese: あたし、もう‥‥何がなんだか わからなくなっちゃって。
English: I... I don't clearly remember what happened then.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: そのときのこと‥‥ 聞かせてもらえるかな。
English: Can you tell us about that?

Japanese: 検事さんは、あたしと青影に 飛びかかってきました。
English: Mr. Marshall jumped on Darke. Just then... the lights went out.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥ 部屋の電気がパッと消えたんです。
電気が‥‥？
English: The lights?

Japanese: ちょうど今ごろの 時期だったんですけど‥‥
English: It was just about this time of year...

Japanese: あの日は、カミナリが鳴っていて ‥‥近くに落雷したんです。
English: There was a terrible storm going on, and lightning struck nearby.

Japanese: それで、停電に‥‥
English: So the electricity went out?

Japanese: しかし‥‥妙ですな。
English: Wait a minute...

Japanese: 部屋が暗かったのなら‥‥ 何も見えないハズでは？
English: If it was pitch dark in that room...
you shouldn't have been able to see anything, right?

Japanese: あの、一瞬‥‥窓のソトで カミナリが光ったんです。
English: Right, but just then lightning flashed again outside.

Japanese: そのストロボのような光が 目に焼きついて‥‥
English: That sudden flash left an unforgettable image of the scene in my mind...

Japanese: なるほど‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: あたし‥‥そのときのコト、 刑事さんにも話しましたから‥‥
English: I told the detective about what I saw then.

Japanese: 刑事さん‥‥？
English: The detective?

Japanese: 多田敷刑事さんです。 ‥‥捜査セキニンシャの。
English: Yes. Detective Goodman. He was in charge of the case.

Japanese: （多田敷　道夫‥‥ 被害者の捜査官、か‥‥）
English: (Detective Bruce Goodman... The victim...)

Japanese: （‥‥あかねちゃん、ツラそうだ。 今は、苦しめたくない‥‥）
English: (Poor Ema. These memories are really torturing her. She's been through enough already.)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 もう、けっこうです。
English: Thank you, Ema. You don't have to go into that.

Japanese: それでは、聞かせていただきたい。
English: Now then, please tell us.

Japanese: あなたの目に焼きついている、 “一瞬の光景”‥‥
English: This scene that imprinted an image in your mind...

Japanese: それは、いったい どのようなモノだったのか？
English: Can you please describe it to us?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: The man...

Japanese: ‥‥多田敷捜査官と、 話をしたんだ。２年前に‥‥
English: So you spoke with Detective Goodman about this... two years ago.

Japanese: はい。‥‥今回の事件‥‥ あたし、コワかった‥‥
English: Yes. That's what's so scary about this trial.

Japanese: ２年前に会った捜査官のヒトたちが ‥‥みんな関係していたから。
（罪門さんに、おキョウさん、 巌徒局長に‥‥多田敷さん、か）
多田敷捜査官には、あなたが 目撃したコトを話したのだろうか？
English: And you told Detective Goodman about what you saw?

Japanese: 話しました。‥‥でも。
English: Yes, but...

Japanese: あたし‥‥そのとき。 うまくコトバが出てこなくて。
English: at the time, the words just wouldn't come out.

Japanese: 絵を描いたりしたんですけど‥‥
English: That's why I drew a picture.

Japanese: （“絵”‥‥か。 前にも聞いたような気がするな）
English: (A picture... Yes, I think she mentioned that before.)

Japanese: いかがですかな、弁護人。 今の証言は。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Have you heard enough?

Japanese: 証人が描いたという“絵”‥‥ 重要なイミを持つと考えます。
English: This picture the witness drew...
I believe it has a very important meaning.

Japanese: しかし！　２年前、証拠品の中に そんな絵はなかったハズだが。
English: But the list of evidence I was given two years ago didn't contain a picture...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。今の発言を 証言に加えていただきましょう。
English: Witness. Would you mind if we added this statement to your testimony?

Japanese: は、はい。わかりました‥‥
English: Y-yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥それは‥‥ 本当に、ショックでしたね。
English: That must have been a real shock.

Japanese: 目をつぶると‥‥ 今でもハッキリ見えるんです。
English: Even now when I close my eyes, I can still see it just as clearly.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで‥‥
English: Tell us...

Japanese: そのとき、あなた自身は どうしていたのだろうか？
English: what were you doing at that moment?

Japanese: たしか、青影　丈にヒトジチに とられていたと証言されていたが。
English: I believe you testified that Joe Darke was holding you hostage.

Japanese: あのとき‥‥カミナリが鳴って、 部屋の電気が消えたとき‥‥
English: When lightning struck and the lights went out,

Japanese: 検事さんが、サツジンハンに 飛びかかりました。
English: Mr. Marshall jumped on Darke. I was thrown aside and the two began wrestling each other.

Japanese: そして、あたしは放り出されて‥‥ ２人は争い始めたんです。
ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あたしは‥‥それを 見ていたんだと思うんですけど‥‥
English: I'm pretty sure I was watching them.

Japanese: きみが目撃した“光景”‥‥ たしかに、絵に描いたんだね？
English: You drew a picture of the scene you witnessed, right?

Japanese: はい。あたし、少しでも 捜査の役に立ちたかったから。
English: Yes. I wanted to do everything I could to help the investigation.

Japanese: ‥‥目を閉じるだけで、 いつだって見えちゃうし‥‥
English: I can still see it now... Whenever I close my eyes...

Japanese: しかし‥‥ それは少し、不自然だ。
English: That's strange... I took over the case after Prosecutor Marshall died,

Japanese: 罪門検事の死後、あの事件は 私が引きついだのだが‥‥
そのような証拠品は、 受け取っていないのだが。
English: yet I never received any picture.

Japanese: 証人の、カンちがいでは ないのだろうか？
English: Perhaps the witness is mistaken?

Japanese: あ、あたし。たしかに描きました！ 気のせいじゃないです‥‥
English: B-but I did draw it! I swear! I'm not just imagining it...

Japanese: （あかねちゃんの描いた“絵”か。 何か、引っかかる‥‥）
English: (This picture that Ema drew... that reminds me!)

Japanese: （‥‥もう一度、証拠品を よく調べてみたほうがいいな）
English: (I guess I should check the evidence again...)

Japanese: それでは‥‥ とにかく、聞かせていただこう。
English: Well anyway. Let's continue.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。
English: Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: この少女が、いっしょうけんめい マゴコロをこめて描いた“絵”‥‥
English: This little girl put all her heart into drawing that picture...

Japanese: あなたは、あくまでも 否定するのですかッ！
English: and yet you still insist on denying its existence?

Japanese: ‥‥えッ！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: わ、私をワルモノにしないで もらいたい！
English: Hey, I'm not the bad guy!

Japanese: 否定も何も‥‥担当検事として 私は、そんな絵は知らぬッ！
English: All I'm saying is that as the prosecutor for that case, I wasn't handed such a picture!

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: That may well be...

Japanese: ジッサイ、ここに こうして存在するのですよ。
English: but that doesn't mean it doesn't exist. Behold!

Japanese: これは‥‥《ＳＬ９号事件》の 証拠品リスト、ですか？
English: This is... the evidence list for the SL-9 Incident?

Japanese: そのリスト、ウラ返して ごらんください。
English: Please turn it over, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥ウラ、うら、と。 ああああああああッ！　これは！
English: Turn it over...? Turn... it... AAAAAAAHHHH! What's this!?

Japanese: な‥‥なんだ、コレは‥‥
English: Yes, what is that?

Japanese: あ！　あたしの 描いた絵です、それ！
English: Hey! That's it! That's the picture I drew!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥２人のオトコが 争っているようです。
English: Indeed... Two men appear to be wrestling here.

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなッ！ そもそも、弁護人ッ！
English: What's the meaning of this!?

Japanese: なぜ、キサマごときが このリストを持っているのだ！
English: What are you doing with that list!?

Japanese: ご、“ごとき”‥‥
証拠品リストは、担当検事が 管理している！　それなのに‥‥
English: Only the prosecutor in charge should have access to that list!
Huh...?

Japanese: この、２枚のリスト‥‥ 内容がちがうようですぞ。
English: These lists... They're... They're different from each other.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと‥‥？
English: What?

Japanese: どうやら、御剣検事‥‥
English: It would appear, Mr. Edgeworth,

Japanese: ２年前の証拠品リストは、 不完全だったようです。
English: that the evidence list you were handed two years ago... was incomplete.

Japanese: このリストは、 ２枚で１つになるようです。
English: These two lists... fit together to form one.

Japanese: ‥‥おたがい、切り取られた アトがありますからな。
English: You can see the marks here, where they were torn apart from each other.

Japanese: つまり‥‥御剣検事。 これで、ハッキリしたワケだね。
English: So you see, Mr. Edgeworth. It's quite obvious what happened.
Two years ago...

Japanese: きみのところに、すべての 証拠品は届いていなかったんだ。
English: only half of the evidence in that case ever reached you.
What...

Japanese: ナニィィィィィィィィィィッ！
English: WWWHHAAAAAATTTTTT!!!!????

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、こんなダイジな リストのウラに絵を描くとは‥‥
English: Who'd have thought the picture would have been drawn on the back of the list...

Japanese: 取調べ室で、多田敷刑事さんに わたされたんです、それ。
English: That was handed to Detective Goodman in the questioning room?
(Wait a minute...)

Japanese: （“半分に切り取られたリスト” ‥‥もしかして！）
English: (If this list was torn in half, then that means...)

Japanese: 裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか、弁護人。 いいトシして、目をキラめかせて。
English: Are you all right, Mr. Wright? Your eyes are bulging from your head...

Japanese: 証拠品リストが、半分に 切り取られたモノならば‥‥
English: If the evidence list was torn in half...

Japanese: 御剣検事のリストのウラにも、 何か描かれているかもしれないッ！
English: then there might be more of the drawing on the back of Mr. Edgeworth's list!

Japanese: た、たしかに！ ‥‥御剣検事ッ！
English: Yes, that's quite conceivable. Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 可能性はあるだろう。 このリストのウラは‥‥‥‥‥む。
English: It's possible. Let's see... MM!

Japanese: むむむむ‥‥む‥‥むゥゥゥッ！
English: MMMMMMMMMMMMM!!!

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしましたかな？
English: Is something wrong?

Japanese: （アキラカに どうかしたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Do you even have to ask?)

Japanese: むむむむ‥‥む‥‥むゥゥゥッ！
English: MMMMM‥‥M‥‥MMMMM!

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイ。
English: Sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: たしかに、私のリストのウラにも 絵が描かれていたようだ。
English: There is something drawn on the back of my list.

Japanese: ‥‥これが、それだッ！
English: This is that... thing!

Japanese: こ、これは‥‥その。 いわゆる、アレですかな？
English: That's that... that thing!

Japanese: きのう、証拠保管室で おどっていた、カレ‥‥
English: That thing that was dancing in the evidence room!

Japanese: おそらく‥‥どこかの刑事課長が、 ラクガキをしたようだ。
English: Apparently the Head of Criminal Affairs used this for his blueprint.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥刑事課の、 あのオジサン‥‥かな）
English: (I guess he was out of scrap paper...)

Japanese: 証拠品《証拠品リスト》が 完全なものになった。
English: The Evidence List has been updated.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、証人。 証言していただきましょうか。
English: Very well. Witness.

Japanese: ２年前に、あなたが描いた この“絵”について。
English: Will you please testify about this picture you drew two years ago?
...Huh? Oh, Y-yes sir, Your Honor!

Japanese: （どうしたんだ？　あかねちゃん。 絵を見て、何か考えている‥‥）
English: (What's wrong with Ema?)
(She seemed to be thinking about something when she saw the picture.)

Japanese: ～茜の描いた“絵”～
English: -- Ema's Picture --

Japanese: この“絵”‥‥あたしが ２年前に描いたものです。
English: This is the picture I drew two years ago.

Japanese: カミナリがまぶしくて、 黒い影しか見えなかったけど‥‥
English: The flash of lightning was so bright all I could see were shadows.

Japanese: このあと、あたし‥‥ キゼツしちゃったみたいです。
English: After that I must have fainted.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいなく、あのとき見た 光景を、そのまま描いてあります。
English: This picture shows exactly what I saw that instant!

Japanese: 一瞬の、カミナリの光が、 目に焼きついたのですね。
English: To think a flash of lightning could burn such an image in your mind...

Japanese: （それだけに‥‥あかねちゃんは “見たまま”を描いている！）
English: (Thanks to that though, she was able to show us exactly what she saw!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、ムジュンは 見あたらないようです。
English: Well, I don't see any contradictions here.

Japanese: 殺人犯・青影　丈が、 罪門　直斗検事を殺害した‥‥
English: This clearly shows Joe Darke about to murder Prosecutor Neil Marshall.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: The defense may now begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: ～茜の描いた“絵”～
English: -- Ema's Picture --

Japanese: この“絵”‥‥あたしが ２年前に描いたものです。
English: This is the picture I drew two years ago.

Japanese: カミナリがまぶしくて、 黒い影しか見えなかったけど‥‥
English: The flash of lightning was so bright all I could see were shadows.

Japanese: このあと、あたし‥‥ キゼツしちゃったみたいです。
English: After that I must have fainted.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいなく、あのとき見た 光景を、そのまま描いてあります。
English: This picture shows exactly what I saw that instant!

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、オカシイ‥‥）
English: (That's strange...)

Japanese: （彼女は、自分の目に焼きついた 一瞬を、そのまま描いたはずだ）
English: (She claims this is exactly the scene that was imprinted in her mind...)

Japanese: （それなのに、この違和感‥‥）
English: (And yet...)

Japanese: （この“絵”は、アキラカに ムジュンしているッ！）
English: (There's clearly a contradiction here!)

Japanese: 絵は、事件が起こって すぐに描いたのかな。
English: Did you draw this picture right after the incident?

Japanese: ‥‥ええと‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: たぶん、２、３日ぐらい たっていたと思います。
English: I think I drew it two or three days later.

Japanese: あたし、しばらくは ショックで、何もできなくて‥‥
English: At first I was in such a state of shock that I couldn't do anything...

Japanese: その間に、捜査班が再編成された。
English: During that time the detective team was reorganized.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈・宝月　巴の 指揮のもと‥‥
English: Detective Goodman was placed in charge...

Japanese: 多田敷捜査官が、捜査責任者に 任命されたわけだ。
English: under the direction of Damon Gant and Lana Skye.

Japanese: （事件から２、３日後‥‥）
English: (Two or three days later...)

Japanese: （それなら、まだキオクが うすれるコトはない、な）
English: (The memory should still have been fresh in her mind.)

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、証人。
English: Excuse me, witness,

Japanese: この絵は、まっ黒に ぬりつぶされていますが‥‥？
English: but can you please tell us why this picture is painted all black?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥助けてくれたのが、 罪門検事だったというコトも‥‥？
English: So at the time you didn't even know it was Mr. Marshall who had come to your rescue?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりませんでした。
English: No... I couldn't see him clearly.

Japanese: カミナリがまぶしかったし‥‥ つき飛ばされましたから。
English: The lightning was so bright... and I was knocked to the floor.

Japanese: “つき飛ばされた”‥‥？
English: You were knocked to the floor?

Japanese: あたし、あのハンニンに つかまっていたんです。
English: Darke had a tight grip on me, but when Mr. Marshall jumped on him,

Japanese: ‥‥そこに、検事さんが 飛びこんできて‥‥
あたし、背中から 思いっきり、つき飛ばされました。
English: I was knocked away.

Japanese: ふり返った瞬間。 あのカミナリが光ったんです。
English: I turned around, and that's when the lightning flashed.

Japanese: （本当に、あぶないところ だったんだな‥‥）
English: (Poor Ema... I'm just glad she wasn't hurt.)

Japanese: ‥‥そして。 この運命の一瞬のあと‥‥？
English: What happened after the lightning flashed?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥犯行の瞬間は 見ていないんだね？
English: You mean, you didn't see the actual murder take place?

Japanese: は、はい。 ゴメンなさい、あたし‥‥
English: No. I-I'm sorry...

Japanese: ‥‥カミナリの光が闇を払うのは、 ほんの一瞬にすぎない。
English: The flash of lightning only drove off the darkness for a split second. Not only that,

Japanese: そして、かよわい少女が キゼツしたのも、ムリはない。
English: but the trauma of the situation understandably caused the witness to faint.

Japanese: そんな証人を、それでも キミは責めると言うのかッ！
English: Do you really need to torture this girl any further!?

Japanese: ‥‥えッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくをワルモノにしないで もらえますか‥‥
English: Hey, I'm not the bad guy here!

Japanese: とにかく、この絵は‥‥
English: Anyway, this picture...

Japanese: クドいようだけど‥‥ホントに、 見たままを描いたんだね？
English: Sorry for asking so many times, but are you sure you drew exactly what you saw?

Japanese: は‥‥はい！ まちがいないですっ！
English: Of course! This is the exact scene!

Japanese: 刑事さんたちから聞いた話に 影響された、という可能性は？
English: It wasn't influenced in any way from your talks with the detectives?

Japanese: 我々が、証人のキオクを ネジ曲げたとでも言うつもりかな？
English: Are you insinuating we somehow manipulated her memory, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: い、いやいや。 そういうイミじゃないですけど。
English: No, no. Of course not.

Japanese: （こういうときのアイツの目、 シャレにならないよなあ‥‥）
English: (I'd better watch out, or he might find some way to cut my salary!)

Japanese: この絵‥‥何も聞かされる前に 描いたんです。
English: I drew this picture before I heard anything from the detectives,

Japanese: だから、何かに影響されるコトは なかったと思いますけど‥‥
English: so I don't think anyone's story would have influenced me...

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 何か、絵にオカシイ点でも あると言うのですかな？
English: Is there something that's bothering you about this picture?

Japanese: え！　そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Huh? Oh, well...

Japanese: 非常にザンネンですが‥‥
English: I hate to be the bearer of bad news, but this picture the witness drew...

Japanese: 証人の描いた、この絵には、 大きなムジュンがあります。
English: contains a blatant contradiction!

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: What?

Japanese: だって、あたし！　今でも、 ハッキリ思い出せるのに‥‥
English: B-but, I still remember it just like it was yesterday!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥指し示して いただいたほうが早いようです。
English: Mr. Wright.
Perhaps it would be faster if you simply pointed out this contradiction for us.

Japanese: 証人が描いた、この“絵”‥‥ 解剖記録とムジュンする点とは！
English: What part of this picture... contradicts the autopsy report?

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく、 このへんだと思われます！
English: Um, I think it's... uh, this part here?

Japanese: ‥‥どうも、ハッキリ わかりませんが。
English: Hmm... I don't see what's so strange about that.

Japanese: それは、絵がヘタなせいです！
English: That's because the drawing stinks!

Japanese: ヒドい。
English: Mr. Wright... How could you... *sniff*

Japanese: 少女を泣かせたツミは、 死をもって、つぐなってもらおう。
English: The act of making an innocent girl cry should warrant the death penalty.

Japanese: （‥‥ヒトのせいにしちゃダメ、 ってコトだな‥‥）
English: (I guess he means I shouldn't shift the blame to others...)

Japanese: 社会ベンキョウは、おウチで やっておくように！
English: Yes, well. So long as the defense has learned his lesson.

Japanese: （もう一度！　解剖記録と 絵を、よく見くらべるんだ！）
English: (I'd better take another look at that autopsy report and this picture!)

Japanese: ムジュンしているのは モチロン、ここですッ！
English: The contradiction of course lies here!

Japanese: “ハンニン”が振り上げている ナイフを、よく見てください。
English: Take a look at the knife the man is holding.

Japanese: なんと！　刃がポッキリ、 折れているんです！
English: If you look closely, you can see its tip is broken!

Japanese: よく見なくてもわかります、 それぐらい。
English: In fact, you don't even have to look closely to see that.

Japanese: でも、成歩堂さん。 証拠品を見てください！
English: But Mr. Wright, look at the evidence!

Japanese: 凶器のナイフの先も、ホラ。 ちゃんと欠けてるじゃないですか！
English: See the murder weapon? Its tip is broken too!

Japanese: たしか‥‥その、折れた刃の先は 被害者の体内から発見されました。
English: If I recall...
the tip of the knife was found broken off in the victim's body.

Japanese: 青影　丈が刺したっていう、 決定的な証拠ですよね！
English: It was the conclusive piece of evidence that proved Joe Darke was the murderer!

Japanese: ‥‥そうはいかないんだよ、 あかねちゃん。
English: I'm afraid it's not so simple, Ema.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、 何がモンダイだと言うのだッ！
English: Huh? Where could you possibly see a problem!?

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。 《背後より一度、刺されて失血死》
English: It's obvious, really. The victim "suffered a single stab wound to the back."

Japanese: 被害者が、１回しか 刺されていないのであれば！
English: If the victim was only stabbed once,

Japanese: ハンニンが振り上げたナイフが、 折れているハズがないのです！
English: then the murder weapon should not yet be broken!

Japanese: ああああああああああッ！ ‥‥これは、いったいどういう
English: AAAAAAAHHHHHH! What's the meaning of this?

Japanese: そのナイフは、犯行の前から 折れていたと考えればよい！
English: Perhaps the knife was broken beforehand!

Japanese: ‥‥そうはいきません！
English: Sorry, but I'm afraid that's not possible.

Japanese: 折れたナイフの先は、被害者の 体内から発見されています。
English: The tip of the knife was found inside the victim's body.

Japanese: はじめから折れていたのなら！ そんなところにあるハズがないッ！
English: If it was broken beforehand, it couldn't possibly wind up there!

Japanese: たしかに、そのとおりです！ ‥‥これは、いったいど
English: That's right! But what does this mean?

Japanese: 刃の先が、被害者の体内から 発見されたのは、ジジツなのだ！
English: The tip of the knife was undeniably discovered within the victim's body!

Japanese: それならば！　その証人の キオクちがいとしか考えられぬッ！
English: The only possible explanation is the witness's memory is mistaken!

Japanese: だからこそ、ぼくは何度も 証人にカクニンしたのです。
English: That's why I asked her so many times if she was sure she remembered correctly.
I believe you were annoyed at the time.

Japanese: “キオクちがいではないのか？” ‥‥でも、そうではなかった！
English: But she was sure she remembered correctly!

Japanese: しかし！　他に、このムジュンに コタエを与える方法など、ないッ！
English: But... there's no other way to explain this inconsistency!

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ １つだけ、あるんですよ。
English: Not so fast, Mr. Edgeworth. There is another explanation.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事。忘れたのかな？ この事件には‥‥
English: Have you forgotten already?

Japanese: “不正な証拠の操作”が あったことを‥‥
English: About a little something called... "falsified evidence"?

Japanese: なに‥‥ッ！
English: You're treading on thin ice, Wright...

Japanese: ‥‥つまり。 この“折れた刃先”は‥‥
English: All I'm saying,
is that this broken knife might be the piece of evidence that was forged!

Japanese: 検察側の“ねつ造”だった！ そう考えることができるのです。
English: You can't deny the possibility!
No...

Japanese: ぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Aaaaaagh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にッ！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: やはり‥‥あの事件の捜査には、 不正があった、というのですか！
English: Are you saying the investigation really was corrupted?

Japanese: ここで、もう一度‥‥
English: Your Honor...

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件があった日の ことをカクニンしてみましょう。
English: Please allow me to once again go over the events that took place the day of the murder.

Japanese: あの日は‥‥警察局と検事局の 式典が行われました。
English: The Police Department and the Prosecutor's Office were holding a ceremony that day.

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事は、その式典で 検事局賞を受けたあと‥‥
English: After receiving the King of Prosecutors award at the ceremony.

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈とともに、 青影　丈の事情聴取にあたった。
English: Neil Marshall questioned Joe Darke along with Damon Gant.

Japanese: その事情聴取の最中、 青影は取調べ室から逃走‥‥
English: During his questioning, Joe Darke fled the room.

Japanese: 罪門検事は、その後を追って‥‥ 青影　丈に刺殺された！
English: Prosecutor Marshall chased after him... and was killed by Darke.

Japanese: この“物語”のどこかに‥‥ “ウソ”がひそんでいるのです。
English: It is my belief that somewhere in this story... there is a lie.

Japanese: ‥‥ふふむむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥‥あたし。 ウソなんかついていないのに。 I...

English: I'm not lying...

Japanese: ハンニンが振り上げたナイフは、 最初から、折れていたんです！
English: The man really was holding up a broken knife!

Japanese: もし、それが《真実》ならば‥‥

English: If that's true...

Japanese: 他に、考えようはない。 これは、凶器ではなかったのだ。
English: then there's no other way around it.
This could not have been the actual murder weapon.

Japanese: “折れたナイフ”が、 他にあったと考えるしかない！
English: There must have been another broken knife!

Japanese: しかし‥‥そんなにツゴウよく、 ナイフが他にある、など‥‥
English: What are the chances of there being two broken knives?

Japanese: （青影のナイフ以外に、 ベツの刃物が存在した‥‥）
English: (Another broken knife besides Joe Darke's...)

Japanese: （‥‥そんな可能性は、あるのか？ “折れた”刃物の《正体》‥‥）
English: (Could there have been one?)

Japanese: （どう考えても‥‥ そんな刃物、あるワケがない！）
English: (Broken knives don't just grow on trees...)
(There's no way there was another one!)

Japanese: どうなのですか？　弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥その。 証人の見まちがい‥‥かと。
English: Your Honor, I believe... the witness must be mistaken.

Japanese: やっぱり、そうなんですね。
English: I knew it.

Japanese: 信じてくれないんだ。 ‥‥あたしのコト。
English: You really don't believe me...

Japanese: （彼女は、この２年間 苦しみつづけて来たんだ）
English: (That flash of lightning burned an unforgettable image in her mind...)

Japanese: （カミナリの光が、 目に焼きつけた“光景”に！）
English: (One that's been torturing her ever since!)

Japanese: （そこに“見まちがい”なんて、 あるハズがない！）
English: (There's no way Ema could be mistaken!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人がここまで主張する以上。
English: If the witness is this adamant about the accuracy of what she saw...

Japanese: “見まちがい”で、かたづける ワケにはいかないでしょう。
English: it can't just be explained away by a simple observational error.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: 彼女は、見たのです！ あの一瞬‥‥折れた“ナイフ”を。
English: In that instant... Ema really did see a broken knife!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥弁護人には、 考えがあるようですね。
English: I assume then,

Japanese: 青影のナイフとはベツの、 “凶器”の存在‥‥
English: that you have some information about this "other" broken knife?
If so, please feel free to enlighten us.

Japanese: （凶器は、《最初から折れていた》 ‥‥１つだけ、可能性はある！）
English: (The murder weapon was already broken prior to the murder... There's only one way!)

Japanese: ‥‥ごらんいただきましょう。 これが“凶器”の正体です！
English: Take a look at this. Here's the real murder weapon!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: If anything's broken here...

Japanese: 最初から折れていたのは、 弁護士自身だった、というワケか。
English: it's you!
Huh?

Japanese: これ以上、イイカゲンな主張を つづけるのであれば‥‥
English: I'm sure this must all be very amusing to you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この私が、ポッキリへし折って さし上げましょうッ！
English: But may I remind you that the fate of Ms. Lana Skye hangs in the balance?

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥ もう一度、おねがいします！
English: S-sorry, Your Honor. Please give me another chance.

Japanese: やはり‥‥コタエは ２年前にありました。
English: The answer lies in the past... Two years in the past.

Japanese: ‥‥この、パネルの中にッ！
English: Right here inside this picture!

Japanese: これは、授賞式の日の写真‥‥ あ、ああ‥‥ッ！
English: This is a picture of the awards ceremony. A-AAAAHH!

Japanese: ど、どうしましたか、御剣検事！
English: What is it, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 折れた‥‥“凶器”‥‥！
English: It's the... the broken murder weapon!

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事が持っている “賞品”に注目してください。
English: Notice the award Prosecutor Marshall is holding.

Japanese: ‥‥《折れたナイフ》が‥‥ こんなところに！
English: That's... a broken knife!

Japanese: 証人の絵に描かれた“凶器”は、 青影　丈のナイフではなかった。
English: As we earlier concluded, the knife in the picture was not Joe Darke's knife.

Japanese: ‥‥それならば！
English: That being the case...

Japanese: その正体は、この《賞品》だった のではないでしょうかッ！
English: the knife the witness saw, was in all likelihood this award!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にぃッ！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: あの日‥‥罪門検事は、《検事・ オブ・ザ・イヤー》に選ばれた。
English: Neil Marshall was awarded King of Prosecutors that day.

Japanese: 賞品として、この《割れた盾》と 《折れた剣》を受け取ったのです。
English: As an award, he was given this broken shield and a broken knife.

Japanese: そして、青影　丈が逃走したとき ‥‥とっさに、それを手に取った！
English: When he chased after Joe Darke, he pulled out this knife.

Japanese: 警察官ではない罪門　直斗は、 当然、ピストルを持っていない。
English: Being a prosecutor, he did not carry a pistol.

Japanese: 《折れた剣》は、イザという ときのための、武器だったのです！
English: This broken knife was the only weapon he had in this dangerous situation.

Japanese: そんな‥‥ そんなコトはあり得ぬッ！
English: But that... That can't be!

Japanese: その理由は！
English: Oh? And why not, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: もし、検事局賞の《折れた剣》が 《凶器》ということになれば‥‥
English: Because if the King of Prosecutors award knife was the murder weapon...

Japanese: 《被害者》と《加害者》が 逆転してしまうではないかッ！
English: then the murderer and the victim would be reversed!

Japanese: なんだって‥‥？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！
English: I mean...

Japanese: ナイフを振りかざしている この“ハンニン”こそが‥‥
English: This man raising a knife... would have been Prosecutor Neil Marshall!

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事だった、という コトになってしまうのだッ！

English: Oh...

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: OOOOOHHHHH!!!

Japanese: しかし！　ジッサイに亡くなって いるのは、検事のほうですぞ！
English: But the prosecutor was the one who actually died!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥たしかに。 （いったい、どういうコトだ‥‥）
English: That's true... (What's going on here?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら。弁護人の 勇み足だったようだ‥‥
English: It seems Mr. Wright has been a bit too eager to jump to conclusions...

Japanese: 待ってください！　あたし‥‥ 思い出しました。すべて！
English: Wait! I... I remember now... I remember everything!

Japanese: しょ、証人‥‥
English: Witness...?

Japanese: あの！　御剣検事さん！
English: Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: なんだろうか‥‥
English: What is it?

Japanese: さっきの‥‥検事さんの 証拠品リスト、見せてください！
English: Could you show me your evidence list again, please?

Japanese: リスト‥‥？　ウラに ラクガキされていた、アレですか？
English: His list? The one with that... picture scribbled on the back?

Japanese: やっぱり、そうだ。
English: I knew it...

Japanese: これ‥‥やっぱり。 あたしが描いたものなんです。
English: This picture... I'm the one who drew it.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: What!?

Japanese: きみが、描いた‥‥？
English: You drew that?

Japanese: あたしが描いたとき‥‥ リストは、破かれていなかったの。
English: That's right.

Japanese: そうです‥‥あたし。
English: The list wasn't torn in half at the time I drew this picture.

Japanese: ‥‥あのときのコト、 必死に忘れようとして‥‥
English: All this time I've been trying so hard to forget...

Japanese: 今まで、ココロの底に 閉じこめていたんだ‥‥
English: I must have locked this part away deep inside me...

Japanese: どうやら‥‥《証言》として うかがったほうがいいようです。
English: Perhaps it would be best... if we added this to the witness's testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥よろしいですか？　証人。
English: Would you please tell us what you've recalled, Ms. Skye?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （ナイフのムジュンに、 ナゾのタイホくんのカゲ‥‥）
English: (First the knife mix-up, and now... the Blue Badger?)

Japanese: （いったい、どんなハナシが 飛び出すんだ‥‥？）
English: (This should be interesting...)

Japanese: ～思い出したこと～
English: -- Ema's Recollection --

Japanese: ハンニンのヒトが、ナイフを 振り上げるのを見て‥‥あたし。
English: When I saw that man raise his knife...

Japanese: ムチュウで、飛びかかったんです！ ‥‥２人の人影に向かって。
English: I panicked, and rushed toward both of them.

Japanese: そして、ナイフのヒトを つき飛ばした‥‥気がします。
English: I think I... I knocked away the man with the knife.

Japanese: そのとき、窓が一瞬光って。 あたし‥‥タイホくんを見たの！
English: Just then there was another flash of lightning, and that's when I saw... the Blue Badger!

Japanese: 部屋の中には、いなかったのに‥‥ たしかに、タイホくんでした！
English: He wasn't in the room, but I'm sure I saw his shadow!

Japanese: ‥‥なんという‥‥
English: This is certainly most unusual...

Japanese: バカな！　あり得ぬッ！
English: Try impossible!

Japanese: 刑事課の課長が、このカイブツを 生み出したのは、今年のコトだ！
English: The Head Detective of Criminal Affairs didn't even design him until this year!

Japanese: （２年前には‥‥まだ、 存在すらしなかったワケか）
English: (That would mean he didn't even exist two years ago...)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいしましょうかな
English: Yes, well. The defense may now begin its cross-examination.

Japanese: ダメ！　これ以上‥‥ 裁判をつづけないで！　おねがい！
English: Stop! Please! Don't pursue this any further!

Japanese: ‥‥ともえさん！
English: Lana!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか、被告人！ 被告席を離れないように！
English: What's the meaning of this!? Please remain seated in the defendant's chair!

Japanese: こんなの、違法よ！　私‥‥ 罪を認めているではないですか！
English: But you can't do this! I've already confessed to the crime!

Japanese: どうして、そっとして おいてくれないの！
English: Why can't you just leave it at that!?

Japanese: ‥‥宝月主席検事。
English: Chief Prosecutor Skye.

Japanese: すでに、ここまで来てしまった。
English: We've already come this far.

Japanese: ‥‥もう、戻ることはできない。
English: It's too late to turn back.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Silence!

Japanese: これより、尋問に入ります。 係官。被告人をおねがいします。
English: The defense will now begin its cross-examination.
Bailiff. Please detain the defendant.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥いよいよ、 核心に近づいたみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It seems we're finally getting to the core of the matter.)

Japanese: ～思い出したこと～
English: -- Ema's Recollection --

Japanese: ハンニンのヒトが、ナイフを 振り上げるのを見て‥‥あたし。
English: When I saw that man raise his knife...

Japanese: ムチュウで、飛びかかったんです！ ‥‥２人の人影に向かって。
English: I panicked, and rushed toward both of them.

Japanese: そして、ナイフのヒトを つき飛ばした‥‥気がします。
English: I think I... I knocked away the man with the knife.

Japanese: そのとき、窓が一瞬光って。 あたし‥‥タイホくんを見たの！
English: Just then there was another flash of lightning, and that's when I saw... the Blue Badger!

Japanese: 部屋の中には、いなかったのに‥‥ たしかに、タイホくんでした！
English: He wasn't in the room, but I'm sure I saw his shadow!

Japanese: （‥‥２年前、タイホくんは まだ、存在しなかった‥‥）
English: (The Blue Badger didn't even exist two years ago...)

Japanese: （じゃあ、あかねちゃんが見たのは いったい、なんだったのか？）
English: (So what exactly did Ema see?)

Japanese: （証拠品はすべて、そろっている。 あとは‥‥そう！）
English: (I've gathered all the evidence. All that's left now...)

Japanese: （それを、“どう見るか”‥‥）
English: (is to put the pieces of the puzzle together...)

Japanese: ハンニン‥‥というと、 青影　丈のコトですね？
English: When you say, "that man," I assume you refer to Joe Darke?

Japanese: たぶん‥‥そうだと思います。
English: Yes... at least, I think it was him.
You... "think"?

Japanese: ‥‥影‥‥しか 見えなかったから。
English: All I could really see were shadows.

Japanese: 検事局の記録にも残っている。 事件の直前の《停電》が、な。
English: The power outage that immediately preceded the incident,
is also documented in the Prosecutor's Office reports.

Japanese: それで、あなたは‥‥
English: So then you...

Japanese: なぜ、そんなキケンなことを！
English: Why would you do something so dangerous!?

Japanese: だって！　あたし、見たんです！
English: What else could I have done!?

Japanese: “ハンニン”がナイフを振り上げて ‥‥刺そうとしているところを‥‥
English: He was about to stab Mr. Marshall!

Japanese: （彼女は当然、ナイフを持っている ほうが青影だと思っていた‥‥）
English: (She seems convinced that Darke was the one holding the knife...)

Japanese: しかし。折れた“剣”を持っていた のは、罪門検事のほうだった‥‥
English: But as we've just theorized,
Mr. Marshall was the one holding the knife.

Japanese: そんなコト‥‥あたし。 知らなかったから‥‥
English: Well, I didn't know that at the time!

Japanese: ハンニンにつき飛ばされたとき、 思ったんです。
English: When that Darke guy knocked me down,

Japanese: “あのヒトを、助けなくちゃ” ‥‥って。
English: all I could think was, "I've got to help Mr. Marshall!"

Japanese: “気がした”‥‥というのは？
English: What do you mean, you "think"?

Japanese: あたし‥‥あのとき。 もう、ムチュウでした！
English: It... it all happened so fast... And I was in shock!

Japanese: だから、よく覚えてないんです。 自分が、何をしたのか‥‥
English: I don't remember everything clearly. What I did... it's all kind of a blur...

Japanese: ほんの数秒の間にイノチを狙われ、 次の一瞬、殺人の現場を目撃した。
English: In a matter of just a few seconds Ms. Skye was almost killed, then she witnessed a

Japanese: ‥‥ムリもないだろう。
English: murder about to take place. A little disorientation is only natural.

Japanese: ハンニンが、ナイフを振り下ろそう としているのが、わかったの。
English: I saw the man about to stab the other person, who I thought was Mr. Marshall.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥ナイフのヒトを 止めようと思ったんです。
English: I knew I had to stop the man with the knife.

Japanese: ソンケイすべき、勇敢な行動です。 ‥‥そして、どうなったのですか？
English: What you did was very brave, young girl. So then, what happened next?

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないんですか？
English: Are you sure about this?

Japanese: だって、ホラ。この絵にも 描いてあるじゃないですか。
English: Of course! See? I even drew a picture of him here!

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: このカイブツを生み出したのは、 刑事課の課長なのだ。
English: the Head Detective of Criminal Affairs thought up this hideous beast.

Japanese: しかも、それは今年に 入ってからのコトで‥‥
English: And that was just this year...

Japanese: ２年前には、まだ存在しなかった。 それは、まちがいないのだが。
English: The Blue Badger didn't exist two years ago. This is all quite verifiable.

Japanese: あたしも、警察に行って ビックリしました。
English: I know it sounds strange. I was surprised too when I saw him at the Police Department.

Japanese: “どこかで見たことがある”‥‥ ずっと、そう思っていたんです。
English: I had this nagging feeling that I'd seen him before somewhere.

Japanese: やっと、思い出しました！
English: Now I finally remember!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥ホント、 ヤッカイなマスコットだな）
English: (Oh brother. Just when you thought that thing had caused enough commotion...)

Japanese: いったい‥‥現場のどこで、 カレはおどっていたのですかな？
English: Tell us... Where in the room did you see him dancing?

Japanese: “カゲ”ということは‥‥
English: His shadow?

Japanese: この、不敵なホホエミを浮かべた カオは、見ていないんですね？
English: So you mean, you didn't actually see his face, with its winning smile and all?

Japanese: はい。でも‥‥今もおぼえてます。 あの、３本のブキミなツノ‥‥
English: That's right... but I still remember it. He had three creepy horns...

Japanese: こんなギロンは、ムダだ。
English: This is pointless!

Japanese: ２年前。まだ“存在しなかった” 以上‥‥見まちがいにすぎぬ！
English: That thing couldn't have possibly existed two years ago...
The witness must be mistaken!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、 見まちがいかもしれません。
English: That may well be...

Japanese: しかし、モンダイなのは、 その“原因”です！
English: but what's important is what caused her to think she saw what she did.

Japanese: それならば‥‥弁護人には ココロ当たりがあると言うのか？
English: Oh? And I suppose you have an explanation?

Japanese: ‥‥この“カゲ”の正体にッ！
English: If so, then by all means... please tell us what this "shadow" really was!

Japanese: （カミナリの光が“一瞬”を 切り取って、目に焼きつけた！）
English: (What was it Ema saw when that lightning flashed?)

Japanese: （あかねちゃんが目撃した、 “タイホくん”の正体とは‥‥）
English: (Who is this "Blue Badger" really...?)

Japanese: そんなの、見当もつきませんね！
English: I absolutely and positively have no idea whatsoever!

Japanese: ‥‥ナニゴトであれ、 ムネを張るのはいいことです。
English: Well, it's always good to be sure of oneself!

Japanese: それによって、敗北のミジメさも 少しはゴマかすことができる‥‥
English: Apparently it helps when trying to overlook one's failures...

Japanese: でも！　あたし。 たしかに見たんです！
English: But I know what I saw! I'm not making this up!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん、おねがい！ あたしを信じてください‥‥
English: Mr. Wright, please! You've got to believe me...

Japanese: あ、あかねちゃん‥‥
English: Ema...

Japanese: （この絵が描かれたとき、 タイホくんは存在しなかった）
English: (The Blue Badger hadn't even been dreamed up when Ema drew this picture.)

Japanese: （‥‥でも。彼女はジッサイに、 この“カゲ”を見たんだ！）
English: (Yet she's certain she saw its "shadow"!)
Ladies and gentlemen...

Japanese: ‥‥２年前の、あの日。 あのオフィスで‥‥１つだけ。
English: It is the defense's belief that on that fateful day two years ago...

Japanese: 《タイホくん》のカタチに “見えた”ものが存在した。
English: there indeed was something that looked similar to the Blue Badger...

Japanese: それが、弁護側の主張です！
English: Something that is now sitting in this very room!

Japanese: 成歩堂さん‥‥！
English: Mr. Wright...!

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人に、 示していただきましょう！
English: In this room? Very well, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: いったい、あの日。あの“瞬間” ‥‥証人は、何を見たのか？
English: What is it that the witness saw in that instant?

Japanese: 宝月　茜さんが見た“タイホくん” の正体とは、なんですかッ！
English: Please show us this mysterious "Blue Badger" look-alike!

Japanese: おそらく‥‥そう。それは、 一瞬のマボロシだったのでしょう。
English: Behold! In her confusion, the witness mistook this for the Blue Badger!

Japanese: ‥‥マボロシを見ているのは 弁護人のほう、だろう。
English: The only person mistaking anything around here is you.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: こんなの、どこから見ても 《タイホくん》にはなりませんぞ！
English: This doesn't look anything like the Blue Badger!

Japanese: も、もう一度。 考えなおしてみます‥‥
English: Oh. Uh, give me a second here...

Japanese: （証拠品を、見なおしてみるんだ！ そう。いろんな角度から‥‥）
English: (I'd better take another look at the evidence... from every angle!)

Japanese: 《タイホくん》の正体は ‥‥‥コイツです。
English: The mysterious "Blue Badger" was in fact... this!

Japanese: それは‥‥‥‥ なんですかな？　いったい。
English: But that's... Er, what exactly is that?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥《ツボ》でしょう。 なんとなく、ハッキリしませんが。
English: I believe it's some sort of jar.

Japanese: でも、成歩堂さん！ カタチがゼンゼンちがいますよ！
English: But Mr. Wright! That doesn't look anything like the Blue Badger!

Japanese: たしかに、このままでは ただのツボです。
English: Indeed it doesn't. As it stands now, it's just a plain jar.

Japanese: しかし‥‥《見かた》を 変えたら、どうでしょうか？
English: However... What if we were to change our viewpoint?
Our viewpoint...?

Japanese: （‥‥提示するんだ！ ツボの“正しい見かた”を！）
English: (I've got to show them the correct angle to look at this from!)

Japanese: （‥‥ち、ちがう！　もう少し、 ソレっぽく見えるハズだ！）
English: (This isn't right... I've got to make it look more like the Badger!)

Japanese: ‥‥今となっては、証人の描いた “絵”こそがすべてだ。
English: Allow me to remind the defense its case hinges on the witness's drawing.

Japanese: そのカタチと完全に一致しない かぎり、我々は認めぬッ！
English: If Mr. Wright can't match the shape the witness drew, we cannot accept his claim!

Japanese: （最適な“角度”‥‥ それを見つけるんだ！）
English: (I've got to find just the right angle!)

Japanese: （タテに回してみたり、 ヨコに回してみたり‥‥）
English: (Maybe I should rotate it vertically a bit more... or horizontally...?)

Japanese: がんばってくださいっ！ 成歩堂さん！
English: Come on, Mr. Wright! You can do it!

Japanese: （‥‥提示するんだ！ ツボの“正しい見かた”を！）
English: (I've got to show them the correct angle to look at this from!)

Japanese: いかがですか？　“キセキ”としか 言いようがありませんが‥‥
English: Well...? Is this a miracle or what?

Japanese: ‥‥だれがどう見ても、 これは、タイホくんです。
English: No one can possibly deny this jar's resemblance to the Blue Badger!

Japanese: そ‥‥‥そんな‥‥‥‥‥‥ バカなコトがあァァァァッ！
English: No... It can't be!!!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、認めなければ ならないようです。
English: The defense has proven its claim.

Japanese: 事件当日、現場にあらわれた 《タイホくん》の正体‥‥
English: The mysterious "Blue Badger" witnessed on the day of the crime was actually this...

Japanese: ‥‥サーカスまがいの曲芸、 楽しませてもらったよ、弁護人。
English: Although we all enjoyed Mr. Wright's dramatic performance, one question remains...

Japanese: しかし！ だから、なんだと言うのかッ！
English: What's your point!?

Japanese: どういうコトですか？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: カイブツの正体は、ツボだった。 それがわかったところで‥‥
English: So that Badger thing was actually just a jar.

Japanese: 何も変わらないではないかッ！
English: That doesn't change anything!

Japanese: ‥‥それはちがうよ、御剣検事。
English: I'm afraid that's where you're wrong, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 《タイホくんの正体が、 この“ツボ”だった》‥‥
それによって‥‥事件は、 まるっきり変わってしまう！
English: You see... this changes everything!

Japanese: ‥‥おもしろい。 では、うかがうとしようか。
English: Indeed? Very well, then. Please tell us...

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間、証人は《ツボ》を見た ‥‥それによって変わるモノとは！
English: What's different now that we know the witness saw this jar?

Japanese: １つずつ、考えていきましょう。
English: Allow me to take these in turn.

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間‥‥証人は、 このツボを目撃しました。
English: At the moment of the murder, the witness saw this jar.

Japanese: しかも‥‥ きわめて特殊な角度で、だ。
English: Not only that, but she saw it at a very specific angle.

Japanese: ‥‥では。それを見ることができた “場所”はどこでしょうか？
English: Knowing this, where could she have seen this jar?

Japanese: ばしょ‥‥
English: Where...?

Japanese: 事件当時、ツボが置かれていた 場所は‥‥
English: The location of the jar is shown in a picture taken on the day of the crime.

Japanese: 写真が物語っています。 巌徒局長のオフィスの、棚の上‥‥
English: It's on a shelf in the office of Damon Gant.

Japanese: バカな！　死体は、宝月　巴の デスク付近で発見されたはずだ！
English: But the body was found lying near Lana Skye's desk!

Japanese: 証人も、そう証言している ではないかッ！
English: The witness testified so herself!

Japanese: しかし！　この写真と、証人の 証言が、真実を物語っている！
English: Yes! And it is these two facts that reveal what actually transpired!

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、２年前。
English: You see...

Japanese: 青影と罪門検事が格闘したのは、 ともえさんのオフィスではない！
English: The struggle between Darke and Marshall did not take place in Lana Skye's office!

Japanese: 部屋の反対側の、巌徒局長の オフィスだったのです！
English: It happened on the other side of the room, in Chief Gant's office!

Japanese: ハンニンが、わざわざ 死体を移動させたというのかな？
English: Are you implying the murderer moved the victim's body?

Japanese: ‥‥巌徒　海慈のオフィスから、 宝月　巴のオフィスへ、と‥‥
English: From Damon Gant's office to Lana Skye's office?

Japanese: そうなりますね。
English: Yes.

Japanese: いったい、なんのためだ！ なんのイミもないッ！
English: Why would he do that!? There's no reason!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: なんの“イミ”もなければ‥‥ そんなメンドウなことはしません。
English: If there wasn't a "reason"... he wouldn't have gone through the trouble.

Japanese: だから‥‥そこには、 “理由”があったはずです。
English: The only logical conclusion... is that there was a "reason."

Japanese: 弁護人には‥‥ その“理由”がわかるのですか？
English: Do you know what that reason was, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら。 やっと、見えてきた‥‥）
English: (I've finally figured it out...)

Japanese: （なぜ、ともえさんがあれほど、 審理を中断させようとしたか！）
English: (So this is why Lana tried to stop the trial!)

Japanese: （‥‥しかし。ここまで来たら、 行けるトコまで、行くしかない）
English: (It's too late to quit now, though.)

Japanese: 証言を思い出してください。
English: Please recall the witness's testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、ナイフを振りかざした 人物を、つき飛ばしました。
English: She said she knocked away the man who was holding up the knife.

Japanese: 一瞬あと‥‥ツボが ハネ飛ばされて、宙を飛んでいる。
English: In the next instant, the jar was hit and flew threw the air.

Japanese: ‥‥その《衝撃》の原因。 いったい、なんだったのでしょう？
English: Now tell me... What could have sent the jar flying?

Japanese: そ。それは、当然‥‥
English: That would have to have been...

Japanese: つき飛ばされた人物が、カベに 打ちつけられたショックでは‥？
English: the impact the man made when he was knocked into the wall?

Japanese: それでは‥‥ もう一度、写真を見てください。
English: Ladies and gentlemen... if I may draw your attention to this picture once more.

Japanese: ツボが置かれたタナのほうへ つき飛ばされたとしたら‥‥
English: If the man was knocked in the direction of the shelf the jar was sitting on...

Japanese: そこには、何があるでしょう？
English: what would he have hit?
A-AHHHHHH...

Japanese: 《ヨロイ》があります！ 《剣》をかまえた‥‥
English: The suit of armor! Holding... a very sharp and dangerous-looking sword...

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 証人に、つき飛ばされた人物は、 《折れた剣》を持っていました。
English: And since the man who was knocked into the armor was carrying a broken knife...

Japanese: つまり‥‥《検事賞》を手にした 罪門検事だった、と考えられます。
English: he would have had to have been Neil Marshall, wielding the Prosecutor's Award.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥‥‥ ちょっと待て、弁護人ッ！
English: No... Mr. Wright...

Japanese: まさか、キサマ‥‥‥
English: You can't be thinking...

Japanese: ‥‥そう、なんです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ここに、もう１つの可能性が 浮かび上がってきてしまう。
English: There is another possibility of what actually transpired in that room.

Japanese: “もう１つの”‥‥？
English: Another possibility?

Japanese: もちろん、証人自身は知らなかった のでしょう。‥‥しかし！
English: Of course the perpetrator would have had no idea, but nevertheless!

Japanese: （うう‥‥コトバに出せない！）
English: (I... I don't know if I can go through with this...)

Japanese: ど。どうしたのですか、弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright? What's the matter?

Japanese: 今、提示された状況を考えれば‥‥ そのイミは、アキラカだ。
English: If events took place as the defense theorizes... Then the outcome is obvious.

Japanese: ‥‥あの瞬間。
English: In that moment...

Japanese: 宝月　茜がつき飛ばしたのが、 罪門　直斗検事だったのならば！
English: assuming the man Ema Skye knocked away was actually Prosecutor Neil Marshall...

Japanese: あ‥‥あたし‥‥が‥‥
English: You mean...

Japanese: 罪門さんが死んだのは‥‥ ‥‥あたしのせい‥‥
English: Mr. Marshall died... because of... me...?

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああああ ああああああああああああああッ！
English: NOOOOOOOOOO!!!

Japanese: ‥‥どさっ‥‥
English: *thud*

Japanese: まさか‥‥彼女の証言が、 こんな結果をもたらすとは‥‥
English: I never imagined her testimony would lead to this...

Japanese: 被害者のイノチを奪ったのは、 証人自身だった‥‥
English: So it was the witness who took the victim's life...

Japanese: そして、それを立証したのも ‥‥証人自身！
English: and then proved so with her own testimony!

Japanese: まさに、前代未聞です！
English: This is unprecedented!

Japanese: ‥‥い、今になって‥‥ 何を言い出すのですか！
English: What... What are you saying!?

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけありません。 でも。状況を考えると‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Ms. Skye, but given the circumstances...

Japanese: 罪門検事を殺害したのは、 あの青影　丈なのです！
English: Joe Darke murdered Prosecutor Marshall!

Japanese: 妹が‥‥あかねが！ その罪を着せられる、なんて‥‥
English: How can you think it was Ema!? How dare you try to pin the crime on her!?

Japanese: ヒニクなコト、だな。
English: Imagine that, coming from you.

Japanese: 《２年前、“ねつ造”があった》 それを認めたのは、あなた自身だ。
English: If you recall, it was you who admitted to forging evidence two years ago.

Japanese: ‥‥罪門検事の死体が 移動させられたのは‥‥
English: The reason you moved Prosecutor Marshall's body...

Japanese: ‥‥宝月　茜の“犯行”を かばうため、だったワケだろうか？
English: was to keep anyone else from finding out about what Ema did, wasn't it?

Japanese: くだらないオシャベリは、 もうやめてッ！
English: I assure you, Mr. Edgeworth, I have no idea what you're talking about.
If you hope to have anyone believe your insane allegations,

Japanese: そこまで言うのならば‥‥ 私は、証拠の提示を求めます！
English: I'm afraid you're going to have to have proof.
Tell me...

Japanese: 妹が、罪門検事を“殺害”した‥‥ その、決定的な証拠をッ！
English: Do you have any conclusive evidence that proves my sister killed Neil Marshall?

Japanese: しょ、証拠‥‥ですか！
English: E-evidence?

Japanese: 今さら、証明できるはずが ないでしょうけどね‥‥
English: I'm willing to bet you don't.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥証拠による立証は ムズカシイでしょうな。
English: Yes... it certainly would be difficult to prove this with evidence.

Japanese: 証拠がダメならば‥‥ “証言”しかない、だろう。
English: If we don't have evidence,
then we'll have to rely on testimony.

Japanese: ザンネンですけれど。 それもムリ、みたいね。
English: I'm afraid that won't work in this case.

Japanese: 事件の当事者は全員、すでに 死んでしまっているのですから。
English: Both parties involved in the incident are dead.

Japanese: （‥‥今となっては、死者に 証言を求めることはできない）
English: (We certainly can't get dead people to testify.)

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ムチャな スイリだったのか‥‥！）
English: (This has all been a wild goose chase from the beginning!)

Japanese: ふう‥‥やれやれ。
English: Hmph. Touche, Ms. Skye.

Japanese: そうなれば‥‥もちろん。 残る可能性は、１つしかない。
English: Of course... That only leaves us with one possibility.

Japanese: ‥‥！ （まだ、あるのか？　可能性が！）
English: (You mean, there's still another possibility?)

Japanese: 御剣検事！　それは、いったい‥‥
English: What do you mean, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: 死者が、すでにメッセージを 残している、という可能性だ。
English: I mean, the possibility that the victim has left us a message.

Japanese: 幸か不幸か、罪門検事は 即死ではなかった。
English: For better or for worse, Mr. Marshall did not die instantly.

Japanese: 自らのイノチを断った人物の 名前を、残したかもしれぬ。
English: He may have left behind the name of the person who took his life...

Japanese: ‥‥なんらかの方法で。
English: somehow.

Japanese: そ、そんな、バカげた‥‥ あり得ないわ！
English: That's... That's impossible!

Japanese: いかがですかな？　弁護人。 これが、最後の可能性です。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? This is the only possibility left to you.

Japanese: 《死者のメッセージ》‥‥ そのようなものが、存在するのか？
English: A message from the deceased... Does such a message exist?

Japanese: （もう一度‥‥法廷記録を よく思い出してみるんだ！）
English: (I've got to think back to the Court Record!)

Japanese: 被害者が残したかもしれない、 《真犯人の名前》は‥‥
English: The real murderer's name that the victim may have left behind...

Japanese: （死者が、ハンニンの名前を 語るなんて‥‥考えられない！）
English: (There's no way a dead person could tell the murderer's name!)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ ここまで、のようですな。
English: Well. It looks like this is as far as we can go with this.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: やはり‥‥まだまだ、だな。 感情に目をくもらされるとは。
English: You disappoint me.
I never thought you the type to let feelings cloud your judgment.

Japanese: 感情に‥‥？
English: My feelings...?

Japanese: 最後のチカラをふりしぼって 残された、死者のコトバ。
English: If we overlook the victim's message...

Japanese: 我々が読みとらなければ‥‥ すべては闇の中なのだッ！
English: one he would have written with literally his last breath,
then everything will be lost in darkness!

Japanese: （やはり‥‥“あれ”は 手がかりだったのか‥‥）
English: (Perhaps that thing really is the clue I'm looking for.)

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら）
English: (This is it.)

Japanese: （今さら、目をそらすことは ユルされないみたいだな）
English: (I can't afford any more mistakes.)

Japanese: 死者の残した“メッセージ”‥‥ 我々はすでに、手にしています。
English: This "message" from the deceased... is already in our possession.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂弁護士！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: そこまでして‥‥あなたは、 妹を殺人者にしたいのですか！
English: Will you stop at nothing to prove my sister a murderer?

Japanese: ゴカイしてもらっては困るな、 主席検事殿。
English: Do not be mistaken, Ms. Skye.

Japanese: 我々は、宝月　茜に、罪を 着せようとしているのではない。
English: Our purpose is not to accuse Ema of any crime.

Japanese: わかっているはずだ。 我々のシゴトは、ただ１つ。
English: There is only one thing we seek:

Japanese: ‥‥《真実》を見つけだす。
English: the truth.

Japanese: それが、どんなに ツラいものであっても‥‥だ。
English: No matter how painful it may be.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人。 証拠の提示を求めます。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 死者の残した《メッセージ》‥‥ それを示す、証拠品とはッ！
English: Please show us the piece of evidence... that conveys a message from the deceased!

Japanese: これこそが、 死者からのメッセージですッ！
English: This is the message from the deceased!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。 わかりました。
English: Right...

Japanese: それでは。これが、 私からのメッセージです。
English: Now then. This is the message from the judge!

Japanese: うううう‥‥ （どうやら、ちがったらしいな）
English: Oh... (I guess that wasn't right.)

Japanese: （死者がメッセージを残すと したら、その“方法”は‥‥）
English: (If a dead person left behind a message...

Japanese: （何かに“書き残した”‥‥ そう考えるのが、自然だろうな）
English: it would have to be in written form. That's the only logical conclusion.)

Japanese: キサマ自身が死者にならぬよう、 メッセージには気をつけるコトだ。
English: You'd better be careful, or you might wind up deceased yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、 うかがいましょう。
English: Let's try this again.

Japanese: これこそが、 死者からのメッセージですッ！
English: This is the message left by the deceased.

Japanese: これは‥‥先ほどの 《タイホくん》ですね？
English: This is that "Blue Badger" from before, right?

Japanese: カレが、ハンニンの名を語る、 というコトかしら？
English: Oh, is he going to just speak the killer's name?

Japanese: ‥‥そのカイブツに、 コトバがあるものなら、な。
English: If that thing could, I'm sure it would.

Japanese: （‥‥みんな、コレがツボに 見えなくなっているみたいだな）
English: (Looks like everyone's forgotten this is just a jar.)

Japanese: メッセージは残されていました。 ‥‥このツボの、表面に。
English: A message was left here... on the surface of this jar.

Japanese: どういうことですかな？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: このツボには、かすかに 血のアトが残っています。
English: If you look closely, you can see a faint trail of blood on this jar.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ダレかに ふき取られたようにも見えるが。
English: It looks like someone wiped the blood away.

Japanese: しかし。
English: Yes, but notice:

Japanese: カケラの一部分だけは、なぜか 血がふきとられずに残っています。
English: for some reason... the blood on some of the fragments was not wiped away.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥アキラカに血で 描かれた“線”があります！
English: Yes... there is a line here... drawn in blood!

Japanese: つまり‥‥こういうコト、か。
English: So what you're saying,

Japanese: これらの《点》は‥‥ かつては《線》だった。
English: is these "dots"... were once lines!

Japanese: ‥‥罪門検事は、 即死ではありませんでした。
English: Prosecutor Marshall did not die instantly.

Japanese: だから、自らの血を使って メッセージを残すことができた。
English: He used the few precious moments left to him to leave behind a message!

Japanese: 何者かが、ぬぐい去ろうと したようだが‥‥
English: One that someone apparently wiped away...

Japanese: “線”が折れる部分だけは、ツボに 深くしみこんでしまったのだろう。
English: But blood must have seeped into the jar where the lines change directions.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり！ その点をつなげれば‥‥
English: Precisely so! All we need to do is connect these points...

Japanese: 死者のメッセージが 浮かび上がってくるのです！
English: and the victim's message will become apparent!

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんてこと‥‥‥
‥‥弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 被害者は、どんなメッセージを 我々に残していたのですかッ！
English: What kind of message did the victim leave for us!?

Japanese: それは‥‥この《血痕》が 語ってくれるでしょう！
English: Your Honor... I believe these blood stains will reveal to us the answer!

Japanese: （“点”と“点”をつないで、 文字を作るんだ‥‥）
English: (I've got to connect these dots to make letters.)

Japanese: （あの状況で、被害者が書き残す とすれば‥‥それは、１つ！）
English: (There's only one thing the victim would have written, given the circumstances:)

Japanese: （‥‥“ハンニンの名前”しか ないはずだ！）
English: (The murderer's name!)

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか！ こんなのが書けましたッ！
English: What do you think!? I came up with this message!

Japanese: そうですな‥‥
English: Hmm... Yes...

Japanese: ダメな生徒を持った、 お習字教室のセンセイの気分です。
English: I feel like I'm teaching handwriting to a student who is all thumbs...

Japanese: 《もうしません》と１００回 書くことをオススメしよう。
English: I think you should make him write out "I won't do it again" 100 times.

Japanese: （ニガテなんだよな、漢字。 法廷記録、見てみるか‥‥）
English: (I never was much good at handwriting... I guess I'll check the court record...)

Japanese: （依頼人の無実を立証するのが、 弁護士たちの任務だ）
English: (It's a defense attorney's duty to prove his client's innocence.)

Japanese: （だから‥‥ともえさんを 救うことだけ、考えてきた）
English: (That's why all I've been thinking about is saving Lana.)

Japanese: （その結果、たどりついた コタエが‥‥‥これなのかッ！）
English: (After all my efforts, I never thought it would turn out like this!)

Japanese: ‥‥これが、罪門検事が残した 最後のコトバ、というワケか‥‥
English: So this is the final message Prosecutor Marshall left behind.

Japanese: なんというコトでしょう‥‥
English: Of all people...

Japanese: 殺意はなかった。 ‥‥しかし。
English: She may not have meant it... but in the end, the one who took the victim's life...

Japanese: 結果的に、被害者の イノチをうばったのは‥‥
宝月　茜さん、だったのです。
English: was Ema Skye.

Japanese: だから言ったでしょ？ ‥‥御剣ちゃん。
English: See, Worthy? Can't say I didn't warn you.

Japanese: が‥‥‥巌徒局長‥‥
English: Chief Gant...

Japanese: キミさ。わかってる？ 自分のやったコトの、イミ。
English: Do you understand the implications of what you've done?

Japanese: ど‥‥‥ どういうコトだろうか‥‥？
English: What...? What are you talking about?

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件。 青影　丈って、処刑されたでしょ？
English: Two years ago, Joe Darke was sentenced to death.

Japanese: 裁かれたのは、カレの最後の犯行。 検事は、たしか‥‥キミ、だっけ？
English: He was convicted because of his final murder.
I believe you were the prosecutor in the case, were you not?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥そうなのよ、御剣ちゃん。
English: Yes, Worthy.

Japanese: キミ。無実のオトコをさ。 チョイと処刑させちゃったワケ。
English: Because of you... an innocent man was sentenced to death.

Japanese: しかも‥‥。ねつ造された 証拠を使って、ねェ‥‥
English: Not only that... but you used forged evidence to ensure his conviction!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥‥ぐッ！ ぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: K-kkckck! KKCKRRAAAAHHHH!!!

Japanese: 青影　丈は、連続殺人犯だった！ それは、たしかなハズでしょう！
English: But Joe Darke really was a serial murderer! That's undeniable!

Japanese: そんなコトはね。 モンダイじゃないの。
English: I'm afraid that's not important.

Japanese: 知ってた？　ボクたち、 正義の味方じゃないんだよ。
English: Didn't you know? We aren't defenders of justice.
What?

Japanese: ボクたちはね。法の番人なワケ。
English: We're merely keepers of the law.

Japanese: ひとりのオトコを処刑する‥‥ それって、タイヘンなコトよね。
English: Sentencing a man to death... is no light matter.

Japanese: 現場に“ねつ造”があろうと “インペイ”があろうと‥‥
English: Even if there wasn't any cover-up or evidence forgery...

Japanese: 最終的なセキニンは、 担当した検事にあるのよ。
English: ultimately the responsibility falls on the prosecutor in charge.

Japanese: これだけはね。 動かせないジジツなのさ！
English: Despite what anyone may say, this fact cannot be denied.

Japanese: ‥‥検事局って、 いったいどうなってるんだよ！
English: What's going on at the Prosecutor's Office!?

Japanese: ‥‥ムジツのヤツが 処刑されたかもしれないんだぞ！
English: They might have sent an innocent man to his death!

Japanese: ‥‥あのヒト、どうして 平気なカオ、してられるの！
English: How can he just stand there, like it wasn't his fault!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 静粛にィィィィィィィィィッ！
English: Order! Order! Order! ORDEEEEERRRRRR!!!

Japanese: ‥‥法廷内に、むなしく 木槌の音がひびきわたった‥‥
English: The gavel's pounding fell on deaf ears...

Japanese: ‥‥さわぎはおさまらず、 審理は一時、中断された‥‥
English: Unable to settle the crowd, the judge declared a recess.

Japanese: ‥‥法廷のゆくえは‥‥ もう、ダレにもわからない‥‥
English: Where this trial is headed, no one knows...

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough! This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: The defense has failed to give the court sufficient reason to doubt the prosecution's claim!

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
被告人、宝月　巴に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Ms. Lana Skye...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
